Description: Official institution name as reported in CDS Section A1.
Why (Design Rationale): Display, search, tab header

Description: 6-digit NCES IPEDS unique identifier for the institution; joins to external datasets.
Why (Design Rationale): Canonical key — links to Common App, College Board, Net Price Calculator

Description: Academic year covered by this CDS (e.g. '2024-2025'); format YYYY-YYYY.
Why (Design Rationale): Data currency warning when record is stale

Description: Parser self-assessment of extraction quality: 'high' | 'medium' | 'low'.
Why (Design Rationale): Data quality badge shown to counselors

Description: Original PDF filename, retained for audit trail and re-parsing.
Why (Design Rationale): Internal parser audit — trace any value back to source PDF

Description: Street address of the institution from CDS A1.
Why (Design Rationale): Display field on school card — street address from CDS A1

Description: City parsed from A1 'City/State/Zip/Country' line.
Why (Design Rationale): Display + filter — 'colleges in Boston', map pins, regional grouping

Description: 2-letter US state code (e.g. 'MA') parsed from A1.
Why (Design Rationale): Display + filter — 'colleges in MA', in-state tuition logic for residents

Description: 5-digit or ZIP+4 postal code; kept as string to preserve leading zeros.
Why (Design Rationale): Geocoding fallback, commute-distance calculations for student fit

Description: Country name; defaults to 'United States' since CDS is US-only.
Why (Design Rationale): Future-proofing — CDS is US-only today, but supports international expansion

Description: School home page URL from A1 'WWW Home Page Address'.
Why (Design Rationale): School card link-out — CDS A1 'WWW Home Page Address'. Counselors and students click through to verify info at the source.

Description: CDS section labels (e.g. 'C9', 'H2') the parser could not locate in the source PDF.
Why (Design Rationale): Counselor tooltip: 'GPA data not reported by this school'

Description: Total number of first-time, first-year applicants for Fall enrollment (C1).
Why (Design Rationale): Funnel chart top bar

Description: Total number of first-time, first-year applicants admitted (C1).
Why (Design Rationale): Funnel chart; acceptance rate source

Description: Total number of admitted first-time, first-year students who enrolled (C1).
Why (Design Rationale): Funnel chart; yield rate source

Description: Derived: admitted_total / applicants_total; decimal between 0 and 1.
Why (Design Rationale): Fit scoring, reach/match/safety label, school card

Description: Derived: enrolled_total / admitted_total; decimal between 0 and 1.
Why (Design Rationale): 'Dream vs reality' signal; how badly students want this school

Description: Whether the school offers a binding Early Decision plan (C21).
Why (Design Rationale): Strategy engine — ED available?

Description: Whether the school offers a non-binding Early Action plan (C22).
Why (Design Rationale): Strategy engine — EA available?

Description: Whether the Early Action plan is restrictive (SCEA/REA), limiting other early applications.
Why (Design Rationale): SCEA/REA warning in strategy tab

Description: Early Action application deadline as month/day (e.g. '11/1').
Why (Design Rationale): Application timeline feature

Description: Date students are notified of EA decision as month/day (e.g. '12/16').
Why (Design Rationale): Timeline display — useful but not blocking

Description: Importance of high-school course rigor in admission decisions: very_important | important | considered | not_considered.
Why (Design Rationale): Core of AI fit analysis and 'How to Increase My Chance' tabs

Description: Importance of academic GPA in admission decisions.
Why (Design Rationale): Core of AI fit analysis and 'How to Increase My Chance' tabs

Description: Importance of the application essay in admission decisions.
Why (Design Rationale): Core of AI fit analysis and 'How to Increase My Chance' tabs

Description: Importance of letters of recommendation in admission decisions.
Why (Design Rationale): Core of AI fit analysis and 'How to Increase My Chance' tabs

Description: Importance of extracurricular activities in admission decisions.
Why (Design Rationale): Core of AI fit analysis and 'How to Increase My Chance' tabs

Description: Importance of character and personal qualities in admission decisions.
Why (Design Rationale): Core of AI fit analysis and 'How to Increase My Chance' tabs

Description: Importance of specific talents or abilities (arts, athletics, etc.) in admission decisions.
Why (Design Rationale): Core of AI fit analysis and 'How to Increase My Chance' tabs

Description: Importance of the interview in admission decisions.
Why (Design Rationale): Core of AI fit analysis and 'How to Increase My Chance' tabs

Description: Importance of first-generation college status in admission decisions.
Why (Design Rationale): Contextual fit signal — critical for public school OOS students

Description: Importance of state residency in admission decisions.
Why (Design Rationale): Contextual fit signal — critical for public school OOS students

Description: Importance of geographical residence (region/state/country) in admission decisions.
Why (Design Rationale): Contextual fit signal — critical for public school OOS students

Description: Importance of alumni relationship (legacy) in admission decisions.
Why (Design Rationale): Contextual fit signal — critical for public school OOS students

Description: Importance of SAT/ACT or other standardized test scores in admission decisions.
Why (Design Rationale): Core C7 factor — affects strategy advice

Description: Importance of high-school class rank in admission decisions.
Why (Design Rationale): Not Considered at Harvard — still a signal to students

Description: Importance of religious affiliation/commitment in admission decisions.
Why (Design Rationale): Not Considered at Harvard — still a signal to students

Description: Importance of demonstrated interest (visits, contacts) in admission decisions.
Why (Design Rationale): Not Considered at Harvard — still a signal to students

Description: Importance of paid work experience in admission decisions.
Why (Design Rationale): Considered at Harvard — part of holistic review

Description: Importance of volunteer work in admission decisions.
Why (Design Rationale): Not Used at Harvard — absence is itself a signal; keep all 18

Description: SAT/ACT policy: required | recommended | considered_not_required | test_blind | neither_required_nor_recommended.
Why (Design Rationale): Profile display; gates strategy advice (required vs optional)

Description: 25th percentile of enrolled-student SAT composite (derived from ERW + Math); range 400–1600.
Why (Design Rationale): Score range bar; fit scoring vs student SAT

Description: 75th percentile of enrolled-student SAT composite (derived from ERW + Math); range 400–1600.
Why (Design Rationale): Score range bar; fit scoring vs student SAT

Description: 25th percentile of enrolled-student ACT composite; range 1–36.
Why (Design Rationale): Score range bar; fit scoring vs student ACT

Description: 75th percentile of enrolled-student ACT composite; range 1–36.
Why (Design Rationale): Score range bar; fit scoring vs student ACT

Description: 25th percentile SAT Evidence-Based Reading & Writing sub-score; range 200–800.
Why (Design Rationale): Sub-score detail — useful for score improvement advice

Description: 75th percentile SAT Evidence-Based Reading & Writing sub-score; range 200–800.
Why (Design Rationale): Sub-score detail — useful for score improvement advice

Description: 25th percentile SAT Math sub-score; range 200–800.
Why (Design Rationale): Sub-score detail — useful for score improvement advice

Description: 75th percentile SAT Math sub-score; range 200–800.
Why (Design Rationale): Sub-score detail — useful for score improvement advice

Description: Percentage of enrolled freshmen who submitted SAT scores (0–100).
Why (Design Rationale): Context for test-optional strategy; not used in fit score

Description: Percentage of enrolled freshmen who submitted ACT scores (0–100).
Why (Design Rationale): Context for test-optional strategy; not used in fit score

Description: Average high-school GPA of enrolled freshmen (typically on a 4.0 scale; may exceed if weighted).
Why (Design Rationale): Shown on profile card; first GPA signal students check

Description: True if avg_gpa is reported on a weighted scale (allows values >4.0); false if unweighted.
Why (Design Rationale): Context note: 'GPA scale is weighted' disclaimer on display

Description: Percentage of enrolled freshmen with GPA of 4.0 or above (0–100).
Why (Design Rationale): Fit scoring — where does student GPA land in admitted class

Description: Percentage of enrolled freshmen with GPA between 3.75 and 3.99 (0–100).
Why (Design Rationale): Fit scoring band

Description: Percentage of enrolled freshmen with GPA between 3.50 and 3.74 (0–100).
Why (Design Rationale): Fit scoring band

Description: Percentage of enrolled freshmen with GPA between 3.25 and 3.49 (0–100).
Why (Design Rationale): Useful for mid-tier schools; negligible at Harvard (0.6%)

Description: Percentage of enrolled freshmen with GPA between 3.00 and 3.24 (0–100).
Why (Design Rationale): Useful for mid-tier schools; negligible at Harvard (0.17%)

Description: Student-to-faculty ratio expressed as an integer (e.g. 7 means 7 students per faculty member).
Why (Design Rationale): Profile card, preference matching filter

Description: Source of institutional control: public | private_nonprofit | private_for_profit.
Why (Design Rationale): Gates COA display logic (public vs private)

Description: Campus setting: urban | suburban | town | rural. Sourced from IPEDS (not native to the CDS).
Why (Design Rationale): Preference filter (urban/suburban/rural)

Description: Total undergraduate enrollment (full-time + part-time, degree-seeking + non-degree).
Why (Design Rationale): Size preference filter (small/medium/large)

Description: Percentage of first-time, first-year students living in college-owned/operated housing (0–100).
Why (Design Rationale): Campus life preference; shown on profile

Description: Annual full-time tuition for out-of-state (or all students, for private schools), in USD.
Why (Design Rationale): Primary cost signal; COA card; affordability filter

Description: Annual full-time in-state tuition for public institutions, in USD; null for private schools.
Why (Design Rationale): Null for private — critical for public school records

Description: Annual on-campus food and housing cost (double occupancy, 19 meals/week), in USD.
Why (Design Rationale): COA breakdown bar; housing cost filter

Description: Total cost of attendance for out-of-state students; derived = tuition + fees + room/board + books.
Why (Design Rationale): Top-line affordability number; true cost calculator

Description: Total cost of attendance for in-state students at public schools; null for private.
Why (Design Rationale): Null for private — critical for public in-state students

Description: Annual required fees not included in tuition (registration, health, activity), in USD.
Why (Design Rationale): COA breakdown bar — needed to render full cost breakdown chart

Description: Estimated annual cost of books and supplies, in USD.
Why (Design Rationale): COA breakdown bar — required to render all 5 cost bars and verify total

Description: Estimated annual transportation cost; often reported as string ('varies') or integer USD.
Why (Design Rationale): COA breakdown bar — required to render all 5 cost bars and verify total

Description: Estimated annual other personal expenses (clothing, laundry, entertainment), in USD.
Why (Design Rationale): COA breakdown bar — required to render all 5 cost bars and verify total

Description: Percentage of aided students whose financial need was fully met by the school (0–100).
Why (Design Rationale): 'Will my need be covered?' — single most important aid question

Description: Average total need-based aid package for first-time freshmen, in USD.
Why (Design Rationale): True cost calculator; aid comparison across schools

Description: Average need-based scholarship/grant award for first-time freshmen, in USD.
Why (Design Rationale): Grant vs loan split — students need to know gift vs debt

Description: Average need-based loan award for first-time freshmen, in USD.
Why (Design Rationale): Debt projection — useful but secondary to grant figure

Description: Percentage of freshmen determined to have financial need (0–100).
Why (Design Rationale): 'Do I qualify?' context — not blocking any core feature

Description: Free-text description of major affordability initiatives (e.g. income-cutoff free tuition); CDS H15.
Why (Design Rationale): School-specific aid initiative — critical differentiator for students

Description: Percentage of full-time first-year students retained to the second year (0–100).
Why (Design Rationale): Quality proxy; shown on outcomes tab; used in school comparison

Description: Six-year graduation rate as decimal 0–1 (e.g. 0.95 = 95%).
Why (Design Rationale): Primary outcomes metric; school comparison filter

Description: Four-year graduation rate as decimal 0–1 (e.g. 0.85 = 85%).
Why (Design Rationale): Progression chart — useful context, not essential alone
